title: Allow specifying multiple static IPs in `podman network connect`
body:
### Feature request description

Maybe this is a bug, but currently trying to run a command like this:

`podman network connect my_network my_container --ip6 2001:db8::42 --ip6 fd00::42`

will only statically assign the last IP address specified.

The only way to assign two addresses, is to have one flag be `--ip` and the other `--ip6`; like so:

`podman network connect my_network my_container --ip 2001:db8::42 --ip6 fd00::42`

this is especially weird considering `--ip` accepts an ipv6 address without issue.

### Suggest potential solution

_No response_

### Have you considered any alternatives?

Using both `--ip` and `--ip6` flags as described above; but this is obscure, hacky, and only allows for 2 IPs

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body: The backend type is just an array of ip addresses so yes cli wise it should be easy enough to allow multiple options

I would also like to see this option. More specifically, in my use case I have a container which is connected to 2 networks: frontend & backend. I would love to be able to assign an IP to a specific network.

Especially with respect to Quadlets. I would love to be able to specify an `IP=<network>/<address>`

> Especially with respect to Quadlets. I would love to be able to specify an IP=<network>/<address>

That is already support for a long time and has nothing to do with this issue.

https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-create.1.html#network-mode-net

`Network=mynet:ip=1.2.3.4`
Thank you for the link! I had just found a reference to that when your reply came in, but it was not in the systemd-unit docs, so I had not run across it.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
